title: possible race condition in `podman run --rm`
body:
/kind bug

**Description**

I have a workflow for development that includes running a container and then immediately running the container again. I updated podman today and it started failing on the second invocation because of a race condition it seems. For example I run 

```
cosa build qemu metal-bios metal-uefi && cosa buildextend-installer
```

where `cosa` [calls out to podman](https://github.com/coreos/coreos-assembler/blame/bc7ce186ccc73a880a9cff9ded0814b8472cd61d/README.md#L89-L99) with a `--name cosa --it --rm`. On execution of the second command I now often see:

```
Error: error creating container storage: the container name "cosa" is already in use by "a09c40e38c99ea6089382647d4808c59098a5d53c791cb392a8be145cb7146de". You have to remove that container to be able to reuse that name.: that name is ae...
```

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. run consecutive containers with the same name back to back

```
[dustymabe@media fedora]$ sudo podman run --rm --name foo -it registry.fedoraproject.org/fedora:29 echo hello &&  sudo podman run --rm --name foo -it registry.fedoraproject.org/fedora:29 echo hello
hello
Error: error creating container storage: the container name "foo" is already in use by "3d36176acb34edbdb1dab8671ba356cc73ab76e7aba37354a1cc93e04a65d17a". You have to remove that container to be able to reuse that name.: that name is ale
```


**Describe the results you received:**

An error message telling me the container existed when it should not because it was executed with `--rm`.

**Describe the results you expected:**

No error.


**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

Doesn't happen all the time. But have hit it several times today.

**Output of `podman version`:**


```
[dustymabe@media fedora]$ sudo podman version
Version:            1.1.2
RemoteAPI Version:  1
Go Version:         go1.11.5
Git Commit:         a95a49d3038462d033f84ac314ec8a3064a99cff
Built:              Tue Mar  5 13:10:31 2019
OS/Arch:            linux/amd64

```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**

```
[dustymabe@media fedora]$ sudo podman info --debug
debug:
  compiler: gc

  Distribution:

      runc version 1.0.0-rc6+dev
```

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**

comments.body: Note to self: while looking at the code here, I found the backend for `podman export`, and it's very, very broken. Fix that when I get a chance.
Oh, only broken for rootless. Good news, that.

Anyways, here's our race.

We launch `--rm` as part of the container cleanup process, which fires once the container is finished. However, it fires async, and there's no guarantee it completes before the main Podman process exits.

We need to add code at the end of `podman run` (and possible `podman start`? Probably not necessary there, but could be good form) to wait until the container is deleted if `--rm` is specified before exiting. Not necessary for non-`rm` (the cleanup process still fires, but all it's doing is unmounting the container, so it's no big deal if something else happens immediately thereafter... If the container restarts, cleanup fires afterwards, finds that it's running, exits instead).
Ought to be possible to use events to do this, now that events has landed.

> Note to self: while looking at the code here, I found the backend for `podman export`, and it's very, very broken. Fix that when I get a chance.

we recently fixed an issue with rootless `podman export`.  What issue are you still seeing?

We could probably just delete the container before exiting and race with `cleanup` in doing that, one of the two will fail with `ErrNoSuchCtr`, since we don't catch the errors for both of them so we should be fine.  What do you think?  I've tried locally and it seems to work fine.  I can open a PR if you are fine with this solution, the branch is here: https://github.com/giuseppe/libpod/pull/new/fix-race-rm
I am fine with that solution.
